fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies the clamping logic when clamp is False, but it should apply when clamp is True. As a result, clamping never happens when requested.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__1 == min(max(y__1, min(y0, y1)), max(y0, y1)))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the clamping branch should have been entered and y should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]

spec.expr: not clamp or (min(y0, y1) <= y__3 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True and we fall into the else branch (no clamping applied), y__3 should still be within bounds — but it won't be for extrapolation cases

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (min(y0, y1) <= y__3 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: After y__3 is finalized, if clamp is True it must lie within the sensor temperature range

spec.expr: clamp or (min(y0, y1) <= y__2 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: The then-branch (not clamp) applies clamping, so y__2 must be in range — but this branch fires when clamp is False, which is the wrong condition
